delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This seminar explores themes related to the study of Intercultural Communication, Comparative Culture, and Applied Linguistics, primarily as they apply to language in use. The seminar examines differing communicative patterns and strategies between cultures. There will be frequent and ongoing negotiation of course focus, alternating between language aims (listening, spoken communication, academic writing) and content aims (IC Communication, Comparative Culture, ELT themes) depending on student needs. The seminar builds toward development of research ideas and preparation of an undergraduate thesis proposal, which should include a scholarly review of literature, method of research, and data collection leading to a final thesis product written in English. This course is for students planning to write their undergraduate thesis in English.
aims: This seminar focuses on research into specific aspects of Intercultural Communication and Comparative Culture. Students will be expected to present on their respective themes of interest and occasionally lead seminars by reporting on a literature review and/or research project.
goals:
Development of academic writing based on a process approach
Acquire basic skills for conducting professional research
Develop skills for comparing culture through critical analysis
Conduct a literature review in area of research interest

plan.text: Point-Counterpoint Debate (Student-led project): English as a Lingua Franca
Point-Counterpoint Debate (Student-led project): Task-based Language Teaching
Introduction to academic debate/argumentation II (Reader Responds Forum)
Reader Responds Forum (Student-led project): Incorporating Global Englishes into the ELT classroom
Reader Responds Forum (Student-led project): Teaching English as an International Language
Reader Responds Forum (Student-led project): Challenging the traditional-communicative dichotomy
Reader Responds Forum (Student-led project): ELF concepts, use, and implications
Intercultural Communication Literature Review Project (Intercultural Pragmatics)
Intercultural Communication Literature Review Project (Nonverbal Communication)
Intercultural Communication Literature Review Project (Intercultural Communicative Competence)
Course Wrap and final reports due

prep: Students will be expected to prepare for each seminar and take turns leading class discussions.
